figures.significance: Current Emir of Kuwait since 2023, former intelligence chief who consolidated power by suspending constitutional articles.
figures.biography:
Mishal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah (born 27 September 1940) is the current Emir of Kuwait, reigning since 2023. Born during his father Ahmad Al-Jaber's reign, Mishal spent most of his career in Kuwait's security and intelligence apparatus. He attended Al Mubarakiya School in Kuwait for primary education, then studied at Hendon Police College in the United Kingdom, graduating in 1960.

Mishal served as head of the Ministry of Interior's intelligence and state security service from 1967 to 1980, overseeing the development of the Kuwait State Security service. He later served as deputy chief of the Kuwait National Guard from 2004 to 2020. He became Crown Prince in 2020 and assumed the role of Emir following his half-brother Nawaf's death on 16 December 2023. As Emir, he has consolidated power by dissolving parliament twice and suspending constitutional articles for up to four years in 2024.
achievements: Developed Kuwait State Security service
Served as deputy chief of Kuwait National Guard
Current Emir of Kuwait since 2023

figures.name: Sabah Al-Khalid Al-Sabah
figures.title: Crown Prince of Kuwait
figures.roleDescription: Current Crown Prince and former Prime Minister and Foreign Minister
figures.significance: First member from the Hamad branch of Al-Sabah family to hold the Crown Prince position in Kuwait's history.
figures.biography:
Sabah Al-Khalid Al-Sabah (born 3 March 1953) is the Crown Prince of Kuwait, appointed on 1 June 2024. He holds a bachelor's degree in political sciences from Kuwait University (1977) and started his career in 1978 at the ministry of foreign affairs. He served in various diplomatic roles including Kuwait's ambassador to Saudi Arabia (1995-1998) and chief of national security (1998-2006).

Sabah held multiple ministerial positions including minister of social affairs and labor (2006-2007), minister of information (2007), deputy prime minister and foreign minister (2011-2019), and served as Prime Minister from 2019 to 2022. His appointment as Crown Prince marks a historic first, being the first member from the Hamad branch of the Al-Sabah ruling family to hold this position in Kuwait's history, and the first Crown Prince not from the Jabir or Salim branches since independence in 1961.
achievements: Served as Prime Minister of Kuwait (2019-2022)
Served as Foreign Minister (2011-2019)
First Crown Prince from Hamad branch of Al-Sabah family

figures.name: Ahmad Al-Abdullah Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah
figures.title: Current Prime Minister of Kuwait
figures.roleDescription: Current Prime Minister and former oil minister and central banker
figures.significance: Current Prime Minister of Kuwait since 2024, with extensive experience in finance and oil sectors.
figures.biography:
Ahmad Al-Abdullah Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah (born 5 September 1952) is a Kuwaiti economist, politician and senior member of the ruling family who currently serves as Prime Minister of Kuwait since 15 May 2024. He received a bachelor's degree in finance from the University of Illinois in 1975 and began his career at the Central Bank of Kuwait from 1978 to 1987.

Sabah then worked at private finance institutions from 1987 to 1999, serving as chairman of Burgan Bank SAK. He held various ministerial positions including minister of finance (1999-2001), minister of communication (1999), and oil minister (2009-2011). He was appointed Prime Minister-designate on 15 April 2024 and officially began his premiership on 15 May 2024. He is married with three children and was previously head of the Crown Prince's Court in 2021.
achievements: Current Prime Minister of Kuwait since 2024
Former Minister of Oil (2009-2011)
Former Chairman of Burgan Bank SAK

figures.name: Virginie Kalantarian Mamoulian
figures.title: Theatre Director
figures.roleDescription: Director of Armenian theatre and mother of filmmaker Rouben Mamoulian
figures.significance: She served as director of the Armenian theatre and was from a family of wealthy landowners and financiers.
figures.biography:
Virginie Kalantarian Mamoulian (Armenian: կոյս Քալանթարեան Մամուլեան) was born into a family of wealthy landowners and financiers. She served as a director of the Armenian theatre, demonstrating the cultural prominence of the Kalantarian family in Armenian artistic circles. She was married to Zachary Mamoulian, a bank president, and together they raised their son Rouben and daughter Svetlana in the Armenian Apostolic faith in Tiflis, Russian Empire.

The family was sympathetic to the Czarist regime and fled Russia during the turmoil of the 1917 revolution and the ensuing civil war. Her cultural background and involvement in Armenian theatre likely influenced her son Rouben's later career as a prominent film and theater director. The Kalantarian family's wealth and cultural engagement positioned them as notable figures in Armenian society of their time.
achievements: Served as director of Armenian theatre

figures.name: Petros Kalantarian
figures.title: Physician
figures.roleDescription: Notable physician from New Julfa
figures.significance: He was a prominent physician in the New Julfa Armenian community during the 18th century.
figures.biography:
Petros Kalantarian was born in 1735 in New Julfa, the Armenian quarter of Isfahan, Iran. He was part of the established Armenian community that had been relocated there by Shah Abbas the Great in the early 17th century. As a physician, Kalantarian represented the educated professional class that emerged within the New Julfa community.

The Kalantarian family was among the notable families of New Julfa, which had become a major hub of Armenian trade networks spanning from Amsterdam to Manila. The Armenian community of New Julfa was known for producing merchants, artisans, and professionals who played important roles in international commerce and cultural preservation. Petros Kalantarian's profession as a physician demonstrates the family's involvement in learned professions beyond commerce.
achievements: Practiced as physician in New Julfa

figures.name: Houda Ezra Ebrahim Nonoo
figures.title: Ambassador and Politician
figures.roleDescription: First Jewish ambassador of any Middle Eastern Arab country
figures.significance: She was the first Jew and third woman to be appointed ambassador of Bahrain, serving as Ambassador to the United States from 2008 to 2013.
figures.biography:
Houda Ezra Ebrahim Nonoo was born on 7 September 1964 in Manama, to a family of Jewish business entrepreneurs with origins in present-day Iraq. Her grandfather Ibrahim left Baghdad in 1888 and started a financial business in Bahrain. She lived for an extended period in the United Kingdom, where she attended Carmel College, a Jewish boarding school, and earned an MBA. After her father died in a car accident, she returned to Bahrain to take over the Basma Company, a company offering different office services, from IT to janitoring, becoming a successful businesswoman.

Prior to her appointment to the Majlis al-shura in 2005, she founded and presided over the Bahrain Human Rights Watch Society in 2004, advocating for women's rights and foreign workers' rights in Bahrain. She served as a member of parliament in the 40-member Shura Council for three years, appointed by King Hamad ibn Isa Al Khalifah. On 3 July 2008, she was appointed Ambassador of the Kingdom of Bahrain to the United States, also covering Canada, Mexico, Brazil and Argentina. During her ambassadorship, she made significant changes including transforming iftar gatherings from all-male to mixed-gender events and introducing interfaith reunions. Her term ended in November 2013.
achievements: First Jewish ambassador of any Middle Eastern Arab country
First female Bahraini ambassador to the United States
Founded Bahrain Human Rights Watch Society in 2004
Member of Bahraini Parliament (Shura Council) 2005-2008

figures.name: Ebrahim Daoud Nonoo
figures.title: Businessman and Politician
figures.roleDescription: First Jewish member of Bahraini Parliament's Shura Council
figures.significance: He was the first Jewish member of the Bahraini Parliament's Shura Council and serves as head of Bahrain's Jewish community.
figures.biography:
Ebrahim Daoud Nonoo was born in 1960 and is a prominent Bahraini businessman and politician from the Nonoo family, which originally came from Basra, Iraq over a century ago. He served as a member of the appointed upper house of the Bahraini Parliament's Shura Council between 2001 and 2006, making him the first Jewish member of the council. He currently serves as CEO of the Basma Company and president of the Association of Gulf Jewish Communities (AGJC).

Nonoo plays a central role in Bahrain's small Jewish community, serving as head of the House of the Ten Commandments Synagogue and as the head of the Jewish community of Bahrain. In 2006, he financed repairs to the country's only synagogue, demonstrating his commitment to preserving Jewish heritage in Bahrain. He is related to other prominent members of the Nonoo family, including his niece Houda Nonoo, who succeeded him in parliament and later became ambassador to the United States.
achievements: First Jewish member of Bahraini Parliament's Shura Council (2001-2006)
CEO of the Basma Company
President of the Association of Gulf Jewish Communities
Head of Bahrain's Jewish community
Financed repairs to Bahrain's only synagogue in 2006

figures.name: Misha Nonoo
figures.title: Fashion Designer
figures.roleDescription: Bahraini-British fashion designer
figures.significance: She is a successful fashion designer known for her eponymous women's ready-to-wear line and was the first designer to produce a fashion show exclusively on Instagram.
figures.biography:
Misha Nonoo was born in Bahrain to a Jewish father of Iraqi descent and an English mother. Her family moved to London when she was 11, and she attended ACS Cobham School and Guildford High School in Surrey. During high school, she worked as a fashion model represented by Nevs Agency. She moved to New York City in 2009 and worked in menswear for a tailoring company before launching her own fashion label in 2011.

Nonoo gained recognition when she was spotted at Prune restaurant by a senior buyer from Intermix wearing one of her own designs, resulting in a $150,000 purchase order for her first collection. She was a finalist in the 2013 Vogue/CFDA Fashion Fund and was named to Forbes' 30 Under 30 in Art & Style list. In 2015, she made fashion history by producing the first fashion show exclusively on Instagram during New York Fashion Week. She has collaborated with high-profile figures, including Meghan, Duchess of Sussex, on a clothing collection for the women's charity Smart Works in 2019. She married Michael Hess, an heir to the Hess Oil fortune, in 2019, and they have two children.
achievements: Founded successful eponymous fashion label in 2011
First designer to produce fashion show exclusively on Instagram (2015)
Finalist in 2013 Vogue/CFDA Fashion Fund
Named to Forbes' 30 Under 30 in Art & Style list
Collaborated with Meghan, Duchess of Sussex on charity clothing collection

figures.name: Ibrahim Nonoo
figures.title: Patriarch and Founder
figures.roleDescription: Founder of Nonoo family business in Bahrain
figures.significance: He established the Nonoo family presence in Bahrain by leaving Baghdad in 1888 and starting a financial business.
figures.biography:
Ibrahim Nonoo was Houda Nonoo's grandfather who left Baghdad in 1888 and started a financial business in Bahrain, establishing the Nonoo family's presence in the kingdom. He was part of the wave of Jewish business entrepreneurs from present-day Iraq who migrated to Bahrain in the late 19th century seeking economic opportunities. His decision to relocate from Baghdad to Bahrain laid the foundation for what would become a prominent merchant family that would play significant roles in Bahraini business and politics for generations to come.

Ibrahim's establishment of a financial business in Bahrain was instrumental in the family's eventual success and integration into Bahraini society. His descendants would go on to become influential figures in Bahraini politics and business, with the family maintaining their Jewish identity while becoming fully integrated into Bahraini society over more than a century.
achievements: Founded Nonoo family financial business in Bahrain
Established family presence in Bahrain from Baghdad in 1888

figures.name: Abraham Nonoo
figures.title: Municipal Council Member
figures.roleDescription: First Jewish member of Manama Municipality
figures.significance: He was the first member of the Nonoo family to enter Bahraini politics, serving as a member of the Manama Municipality in 1934.
figures.biography:
Abraham Nonoo served as a member of the Manama Municipality in 1934, making him the first ever member of the first elected municipal body in Bahrain. He was Houda Nonoo's grandfather and represented an early example of Jewish participation in Bahraini civic life. His service in the municipal government demonstrated the integration of the Jewish community into Bahraini political institutions from the early days of formal governance structures.

Abraham's political involvement paved the way for future generations of the Nonoo family to participate in Bahraini politics. His role in the municipality was significant as it showed that the Jewish community, despite being a small minority, was trusted with civic responsibilities and could contribute to the governance of Bahrain during a formative period in the country's modern political development.
achievements: First member of Manama Municipality in 1934
First Nonoo family member to enter Bahraini politics

figures.name: Nancy Khedouri
figures.title: Author and Politician
figures.roleDescription: Bahraini Jewish author and parliamentarian
figures.significance: She is a prominent Jewish Bahraini author who wrote about the Jewish community's history and was appointed to parliament in 2010.
figures.biography:
Nancy Khedouri is a Jewish Bahraini author who wrote 'From Our Beginning to Present Day' about the Bahraini Jewish community, documenting their integration into Bahraini society. In her work, she explained how Jewish men were traditionally traders while women worked as teachers and nurses, developing strong bonds with local citizens. She has been an advocate for religious tolerance in Bahrain and has praised the peaceful coexistence between Jews and Bahrainis under King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa's leadership.

In November 2010, Khedouri was appointed to replace Houda Nonoo in Parliament, continuing the tradition of Jewish representation in Bahraini political institutions. Her family business involves importing tablecloths and linens, making them one of Bahrain's leading importers in this sector. Through her writing and public statements, she has highlighted how Bahrain has practiced religious tolerance and provided peace and security to all its citizens, emphasizing the special relationship between the Jewish community and the broader Bahraini society.
achievements: Authored 'From Our Beginning to Present Day' about Bahraini Jewish community
Appointed to Bahraini Parliament in 2010
Leading importer of tablecloths and linens in Bahrain

figures.name: Sulaiman Al-Fahim
figures.title: Businessman and Television Personality
figures.roleDescription: Self-described billionaire businessman and television personality in UAE real estate
figures.significance: Prominent Emirati businessman involved in major football club acquisitions and real estate ventures.
figures.biography:
Sulaiman Abdul-Karim Mohammed Al-Fahim was born in Dubai in 1976 or 1977 to a merchant family. He obtained his master's degree in Finance and Real Estate from the American University in Washington, D.C., though his claims of having a PhD from the same institution could not be verified. In 1998, tragedy struck when Al-Fahim lost his parents and brother in a car accident.

Al-Fahim became head of the real estate company Hydra Properties, which is owned by the Royal Group, an investment conglomerate headed by Tahnoun bin Zayed Al Nahyan. He gained international prominence for his role in facilitating the Abu Dhabi royal family's acquisition of Manchester City F.C. in 2008, where he signed the initial Memorandum of Understanding. He also briefly owned Portsmouth F.C. for six weeks in 2009. Beyond football, Al-Fahim served as president of UAE Chess Federation from 2008 to 2012 and founded the business reality television show 'Hydra Executives' which ran for two seasons.

However, his career was marred by legal troubles when in 2018, he was convicted and sentenced to five years in jail by a UAE court for stealing £5 million from his wife to fund the Portsmouth purchase. This conviction significantly damaged his reputation and highlighted the risks associated with his business practices.
achievements: Facilitated Abu Dhabi royal family's acquisition of Manchester City F.C.
Founded and hosted business reality TV show 'Hydra Executives'
Served as president of UAE Chess Federation (2008-2012)
Published book 'Brand Builder'

figures.name: Mohammed Al Habtoor
figures.title: Donor and Philanthropist
figures.roleDescription: Major donor to educational institutions
figures.significance: Made significant donation to renovate the Paul Findley Congressional Office Museum at Illinois College in 2007.
figures.biography:
Mohammed Al Habtoor is a notable philanthropist who has made significant contributions to educational institutions. In 2007, he made a substantial donation that enabled the renovation of the Paul Findley Congressional Office Museum at Illinois College in Jacksonville, Illinois. The museum, housed in Whipple Hall on the college campus, is dedicated to former congressman and alumnus Paul Findley and contains artifacts related to Findley's political career, his interest in Abraham Lincoln, and his involvement in human rights and Middle East issues.

Al Habtoor's donation was instrumental in preserving and enhancing this important historical collection, which includes Lincoln's 1837 law office sofa, Findley's congressional desk, WWI and campaign memorabilia, and gifts from seven U.S. presidents and international leaders. The museum serves as an educational resource that is open for tours and visits by appointment, continuing to benefit students and visitors through Al Habtoor's generosity.
achievements: Donated funds for renovation of Paul Findley Congressional Office Museum at Illinois College in 2007

figures.name: Khalaf Ahmad Al Habtoor
figures.title: Healthcare Philanthropist
figures.roleDescription: Major contributor to medical education facilities
figures.significance: Sponsored the largest medical simulation center in the UAE at Mohammed Bin Rashid University of Medicine and Health Sciences.
figures.biography:
Khalaf Ahmad Al Habtoor is a prominent philanthropist who has made significant contributions to medical education in the United Arab Emirates. His most notable contribution is the establishment of the Khalaf Ahmad Al Habtoor Medical Simulation Center, which is located at Mohammed Bin Rashid University of Medicine and Health Sciences (MBRU) within Dubai Healthcare City.

The Al Habtoor Medical Simulation Center represents the largest medical simulation facility in the UAE and serves as a crucial learning resource for medical students. The center provides innovative academic experiences that enrich students' educational journey through advanced simulation technology and resources. It operates alongside other important facilities at MBRU, including Al Maktoum Medical Library and dedicated research and anatomy laboratories, contributing to the university's mission of integrating education, clinical practice, and research in medical training.
achievements: Established the largest medical simulation center in the UAE

figures.name: Rashid Al-Habtoor
figures.title: UAE Businessman
figures.roleDescription: Business executive and hotel industry investor
figures.significance: Submitted a major bid for the acquisition of Leela Hotels & Resorts in India in 2019.
figures.biography:
Rashid Al-Habtoor is a prominent UAE businessman who has been involved in significant international business transactions, particularly in the hospitality sector. In January 2019, he emerged as one of the major suitors for the acquisition of Leela Hotels & Resorts, a prestigious Indian luxury hotel chain. Al-Habtoor, working jointly with business partner Abhishek Verma, submitted a substantial bid of US$600 million for the hotel group.

The bid was part of a competitive acquisition process that included other major suitors such as ITC Group and ultimately Brookfield Asset Management, which eventually won the acquisition. Despite the significant value of Al-Habtoor's offer, questions were raised about the conduct of JM Financial, the asset reconstruction company managing the sale transaction, regarding how various bids were entertained and evaluated. This high-profile attempt to acquire the Indian hotel chain demonstrated Al-Habtoor's active involvement in international hospitality investments and his capacity for major business transactions.
achievements: Submitted $600 million bid for Leela Hotels & Resorts acquisition in 2019

figures.name: Abdullah Saleh Al Mulla
figures.title: Secretary of State and Business Founder
figures.roleDescription: Founder and patriarch of Al Mulla Group
figures.significance: Founded Al Mulla Group in 1938, establishing one of Kuwait's most prominent business dynasties.
figures.biography:
Abdullah Saleh Al Mulla served as Secretary of State and established Al Mulla Group in 1938 by setting up an electrical and domestic appliance store in Kuwait. He soon acquired the GEC franchise from the United Kingdom, marking the beginning of what would become one of Kuwait's largest diversified business groups. Under his leadership, the company expanded into various sectors including automotive, with the creation of Bader Al Mulla and Brothers Company in 1947, which acquired distribution rights for Chrysler Middle East, Plymouth and Dodge Middle East vehicles in Kuwait.

Al Mulla continued to expand the business into marine products, HVAC contracting, travel and industrial equipment distribution. His entrepreneurial vision laid the foundation for what would eventually become a business empire with operations in 8 countries and over 15,000 employees. Following his death in 1955, his business legacy was continued by his sons, with his eldest son Bader taking over as chairman.
achievements: Founded Al Mulla Group in 1938
Acquired GEC franchise from the United Kingdom
Established Bader Al Mulla and Brothers Company in 1947
Secured distribution rights for major American automotive brands in Kuwait

figures.name: Bader Al Mulla
figures.title: Chairman of Al Mulla Group
figures.roleDescription: Eldest son and successor to founder Abdullah Saleh Al Mulla
figures.significance: Led Al Mulla Group as chairman from 1955-1969, continuing his father's business legacy.
figures.biography:
Bader Al Mulla was the eldest son of Abdullah Saleh Al Mulla, founder of Al Mulla Group. Following his father's death in 1955, Bader assumed leadership of the family business as chairman, a position he held until 1969. During his tenure, he oversaw the continued expansion of the Al Mulla Group's operations across various sectors including automotive, marine products, HVAC contracting, and industrial equipment distribution.

After stepping down as chairman in 1969, Bader was succeeded by his brothers Najeeb and Anwar, who took on the roles of Chairman and Deputy Chairman & CEO respectively. The company bearing his name, Bader Al Mulla and Brothers Company, had been established in 1947 and was instrumental in securing major automotive distribution rights in Kuwait. His leadership helped consolidate the business foundation laid by his father during the crucial post-independence period of Kuwait's economic development.
achievements: Served as Chairman of Al Mulla Group from 1955-1969
Continued expansion of family business operations
Maintained major automotive distribution partnerships

figures.name: Najeeb Al Mulla
figures.title: Chairman of Al Mulla Group
figures.roleDescription: Second-generation leader who became Chairman in 1969
figures.significance: Led Al Mulla Group during a period of significant expansion and modernization in the 1970s.
figures.biography:
Najeeb Al Mulla was one of the sons of founder Abdullah Saleh Al Mulla and brother to Bader and Anwar Al Mulla. In 1969, he became Chairman of Al Mulla Group, taking over from his elder brother Bader, while his brother Anwar assumed the role of Deputy Chairman & CEO. This marked a new phase of shared leadership within the family business.

Under Najeeb's chairmanship, the company underwent significant transformation and expansion. In 1972, three years after he took over, the Maseelah Trading Company was founded, which acquired the distribution rights for Mitsubishi Motor Vehicles in Kuwait, leading to dramatic growth in the group's automotive business. In 1978, Najeeb oversaw radical changes to the company structure, including the appointment of a multinational board instead of a board consisting only of family members. During this period, the Group entered additional businesses such as environmental systems, vehicle rental and leasing, fire protection, office equipment, security services, engineering products, consumer appliances and insurance consultancy.
achievements: Served as Chairman of Al Mulla Group from 1969
Established Maseelah Trading Company in 1972
Secured Mitsubishi Motor Vehicles distribution rights
Implemented multinational board structure in 1978
Expanded into multiple new business sectors

figures.name: Anwar Al Mulla
figures.title: Deputy Chairman & CEO of Al Mulla Group
figures.roleDescription: Second-generation leader who served as Deputy Chairman & CEO from 1969
figures.significance: Played a key role in modernizing Al Mulla Group's operations and expanding into new business sectors.
figures.biography:
Anwar Al Mulla was one of the sons of founder Abdullah Saleh Al Mulla and brother to Bader and Najeeb Al Mulla. In 1969, when leadership of the family business transitioned from his elder brother Bader, Anwar took on the role of Deputy Chairman & CEO while his brother Najeeb became Chairman. This dual leadership structure marked a new era for the Al Mulla Group.

As Deputy Chairman & CEO, Anwar was instrumental in the operational management and strategic expansion of the company. He worked alongside his brother Najeeb during a period of significant growth and modernization. Under their joint leadership, the company established Maseelah Trading Company in 1972 and secured important distribution rights for Mitsubishi Motor Vehicles in Kuwait. Anwar also played a key role in the 1978 restructuring that introduced a multinational board and expanded the company into numerous new sectors including environmental systems, vehicle rental and leasing, fire protection, office equipment, security services, engineering products, consumer appliances and insurance consultancy.
achievements: Served as Deputy Chairman & CEO of Al Mulla Group from 1969
Co-led company expansion into multiple business sectors
Helped establish Maseelah Trading Company
Participated in 1978 corporate restructuring and modernization

figures.name: Salem bin Ali al Owais
figures.title: Writer and Poet
figures.roleDescription: One of the most famous early Emirati writers
figures.significance: Recognized as one of the most famous Emirati writers alongside Mubarak Al Oqaili and Ahmed bin Sulayem.
figures.biography:
Salem bin Ali al Owais (1887-1959) was one of the most prominent early Emirati writers and poets. He lived during a transformative period in UAE history, spanning the late pearling era through the early oil discoveries. Al Owais was part of a literary tradition that helped establish Emirati cultural identity through written works.

Along with Mubarak Al Oqaili (1880-1954) and Ahmed bin Sulayem (1905-1976), al Owais formed part of the foundational generation of Emirati literature. His work contributed to the early development of written Emirati culture during a time when the region was transitioning from a pearling-based economy to one that would eventually be transformed by oil wealth. His literary contributions helped preserve and develop Emirati cultural heritage during this crucial period of change.
achievements: Recognized as one of the most famous early Emirati writers
Contributed to foundational Emirati literature

figures.name: Fatima bint Mubarak Al Ketbi
figures.title: Sheikha
figures.roleDescription: Third wife of Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, founder of UAE
figures.significance: Known as the 'Mother of the Nation' and a prominent advocate for women's rights in the UAE.
figures.biography:
Sheikha Fatima was born circa 1943 in Al-Hayer, Al Ain Region, as the only daughter to her parents from a Bedouin and religious family. She married Sheikh Zayed Al Nahyan in 1960 when he was the ruler of the Eastern region, after they met in a mosque. They moved to Abu Dhabi when Sheikh Zayed became the ruler in August 1966, and she became his most influential and favorite spouse.

She is a strong supporter of women's rights in the UAE and founded the UAE's General Women Union (GWU) in 1975. She was instrumental in founding the first women's organization in 1976, the Abu Dhabi Society for the Awakening of Women, and led a nationwide campaign advocating for girls' education. She serves as the supreme chairperson of the Family Development Foundation (FDF) and is President of the Motherhood and Childhood Supreme Council.

Sheikha Fatima is the mother of Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, the current President of the United Arab Emirates, along with seven other children including Sheikh Hamdan, Sheikh Hazza, Sheikh Tahnoun, Sheikh Mansour, Sheikh Abdullah, Sheikha Shamma and Sheikha Alyazia. Her children form the most powerful block in the ruling Al Nahyan family of Abu Dhabi.
achievements: Founded UAE's General Women Union in 1975
Established Abu Dhabi Society for the Awakening of Women in 1976
Received UNESCO Marie Curie Medal for education and women's rights efforts
Launched first National Action Plan on women, peace and security in GCC in 2021
Awarded by five UN organizations in 1997 for women's rights efforts

figures.name: Salem Al Ketbi
figures.title: Dr.
figures.roleDescription: Political analyst, researcher and opinion writer
figures.significance: Renowned Emirati political analyst and researcher specializing in Middle Eastern politics and security issues.
figures.biography:
Salem Al Ketbi is a prominent Emirati political analyst, researcher and opinion writer from a Bedouin background. He holds a Ph.D. in Public Law and Political Science from Hassan II University, Casablanca, for his thesis on political and religious propaganda and leadership through social media in the Arab World.

Al Ketbi has written extensively for major newspapers and media outlets including Gulf News, The Jerusalem Post, Al Arabiya, and many others in both English and Arabic. He focuses on UAE national security, Iranian foreign policy, terrorist organizations and extremist groups. In 2017, he published a political biography of Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan titled 'Fakhr al-Oroba' which won the Arab Women Media Network award for Book of the Year.

He is a member of several prestigious organizations including Chatham House, the International Federation of Journalists, and various press clubs. In 2019, he ran for the Federal National Council election on a platform of education, women and youth empowerment. Al Ketbi has spoken at international forums and is recognized as an expert on Middle Eastern politics.
achievements: Won Arab Women Media Network award for Book of the Year in 2017
Member of Chatham House and International Federation of Journalists
Published historical research on UAE and Palestinian relations
Spoke at 2017 MEDays international forum

figures.name: Ebtesam Al-Ketbi
figures.title: Dr.
figures.roleDescription: Political scientist and founder of The Emirates Policy Center
figures.significance: First Arab woman to lead a think tank and founder of one of the most influential policy centers in the Arab world.
figures.biography:
Dr. Ebtesam Al-Ketbi is a distinguished political scientist who holds a Ph.D. in political science from Cairo University with a thesis on 'The Political Implications of the Rentier State in the Gulf'. She has worked for two decades in analysis and strategic recommendations on regional and global affairs, establishing herself as a leading expert in international politics.

Her most notable achievement is founding and presiding over The Emirates Policy Center (EPC) in 2013, making her the first Arab woman to lead a think tank. Under her leadership, EPC has become one of the most influential think tanks in the Arab world, consistently ranked among the top think tanks in the Middle East and North Africa region according to the Global Think Tank Index Report by the University of Pennsylvania.

Dr. Al-Ketbi has held various positions within regional and global organizations and serves on multiple international advisory boards. In 2015, she was appointed as a member of The Consultative Commission of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC). She has received numerous awards and recognitions, including being listed among The 50 Most Influential Women in the Arab world by Arabian Business magazine in 2018.
achievements: Founded The Emirates Policy Center in 2013
First Arab woman to lead a think tank
Listed among 50 Most Influential Women in Arab world by Arabian Business 2018
Member of GCC Consultative Commission since 2015
Published multiple books on UAE foreign policy and regional politics

figures.name: Hikmat Mizban Ibrahim al-Azzawi
figures.title: Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Finance
figures.roleDescription: Iraqi politician and economist who served under Saddam Hussein's government
figures.significance: Served as Deputy Prime Minister and twice as Minister of Finance under Saddam Hussein's Ba'athist government.
figures.biography:
Hikmat Mizban Ibrahim al-Azzawi was born in 1933 and originally trained as an economist. He was arrested in 1960 for protesting against the government of Iraqi General Abd al-Karim Qasim. He joined the Arab Socialist Ba'ath Party in 1968 and was named undersecretary of state for Commerce. His career faced significant turbulence when he was fired from all his official positions in 1982 and demoted to a low administrative job, being expelled from the party until 1985 when he became Governor of the Central Bank of Iraq. In April 1987, he was publicly humiliated and severely dismissed for refusing to transfer a large sum of money at the demand of one of Saddam's uncles.

President Saddam Hussein forced him back into the position of Minister of Finance in 1995. In July 1999, he was given the honorary title of Deputy Prime Minister. After the United States-led Invasion of Iraq in 2003, he was listed as number 45 (8 of Diamonds) on the Iraqi most-wanted playing cards. He was peacefully detained from his house in Baghdad on April 19, 2003 by Iraqi police and handed over to the US military. He died in prison on January 27, 2012, at age 79, due to lack of his heart medications, with his health deteriorating because of aging and cancer.
achievements: Served as Deputy Prime Minister of Iraq
Served twice as Minister of Finance under Saddam Hussein
Served as Governor of the Central Bank of Iraq

figures.name: Dia Azzawi
figures.title: Artist and Architect
figures.roleDescription: Contemporary Iraqi artist known for incorporating Arabic calligraphy and ancient Mesopotamian art
figures.significance: A prominent contemporary artist whose work includes references to Arabic calligraphy, Sumerian and Assyrian art, and has inspired many contemporary calligraffiti artists.
figures.biography:
Dia Azzawi was born in 1939 and became one of Iraq's most notable contemporary artists. His work is described as contemporary art that includes references to Arabic calligraphy, as well as Sumerian and Assyrian art. Azzawi has been a significant inspiration for many contemporary calligraffiti artists, helping to bridge traditional Iraqi artistic heritage with modern artistic expression.

Azzawi was one of the founding members of Al-Ru'yah al-Jadida (The New Vision) art group, established in 1968 alongside artists Ismail Fatah Al Turk, Muhammed Muhr al-Din, Hashimi al-Samarchi, Salih Jumai'e, and Rafa al-Nasiri. This group promoted the idea of freedom of creativity within a framework of heritage and represented a free art style where artists believed they needed to be true to their own era. He was also a founding member of the One Dimension Group, established around 1971, which sought a new artistic identity drawn from within Iraqi culture and heritage, blending Arabic motifs, especially calligraphy, and geometric shapes into contemporary and abstract artworks. Additionally, he collaborated with Mahmoud Obaidi, an Iraqi-Canadian artist, in building the structure that houses the Nabu Museum in Lebanon.
achievements: Co-founder of Al-Ru'yah al-Jadida (The New Vision) art group in 1968
Founding member of the One Dimension Group around 1971
Collaborated in building the Nabu Museum structure in Lebanon
Inspiration for contemporary calligraffiti artists

figures.name: Souad Naji Al-Azzawi
figures.title: Writer
figures.roleDescription: Iraqi Sunni writer
figures.significance: Notable Iraqi writer from the Sunni community.
figures.biography: Souad Naji Al-Azzawi is listed among the notable Iraqi Sunni writers who have contributed to Iraqi literature. While specific details about her life and works are not provided in the available research, she is recognized as part of the literary tradition of Iraqi Sunni Arabs who have played important roles in the country's cultural and intellectual development.

figures.name: Hazzaa Ali Abdan Khalfan Al Mansoori
figures.title: Astronaut and Former F-16 Fighter Pilot
figures.roleDescription: First Emirati astronaut in space
figures.significance: He became the first person from the United Arab Emirates in space and the first Arab to conduct experiments aboard the International Space Station.
figures.biography:
Hazzaa Al Mansoori was born on 13 December 1983 in the Abu Dhabi suburb of Al Wathba and later moved with his family to Al-Dhafra in western Abu Dhabi. In his childhood, he loved exploring and watching stars and meteors on dark nights in the desert of Liwa where he spent most of his childhood. After graduating from Al Seddique High School in Liwa, his passion for astronomy led him to study aviation at the Khalifa bin Zayed Air College, graduating in 2004. He joined the armed forces and became one of the most outstanding military pilots, eventually becoming the youngest military pilot on the F-16 after training in Arizona for three years.

In 2017, Al Mansoori was selected from 4,022 candidates for the UAE Astronaut Programme launched by Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum and Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. On 25 September 2019, he launched aboard the Soyuz MS-15 spacecraft to the International Space Station for an eight-day mission under the slogan 'Zayed's Ambition'. During his stay, he conducted 15 experiments created by UAE school students, performed Earth observation experiments, and filmed the first ever tour of the ISS in Arabic. He safely returned to Earth on 3 October 2019, making the UAE the 19th country worldwide and the first in the Arab region to send astronauts to the ISS.
achievements: First person from the United Arab Emirates in space
First Arab to conduct scientific experiments on the International Space Station
Youngest F-16 fighter pilot in the UAE
Filmed first tour of ISS in Arabic
Completed over 1,400 hours of astronaut training

figures.name: Hawaa Al Thahak Al Mansoori
figures.title: Physician and Politician
figures.roleDescription: Emirati endocrinologist and Federal National Council member
figures.significance: She was the first Emirati woman to receive a UAE Presidential Scholarship to study medicine in the United States and serves as a member of the Federal National Council.
figures.biography:
Hawaa Al Thahak Al Mansoori is an Emirati physician and politician who was appointed to the Federal National Council of the United Arab Emirates in 2019. She made history as the first Emirati woman to be awarded a UAE Presidential Scholarship to study medicine in the United States. She completed her undergraduate studies at the University of Maryland, receiving a BSc in Neurobiology & Physiology and a BSc in Psychology. In 2001, she was an undergraduate research fellow at the Howard Hughes Medical Institute, working under Sarah Tishkoff.

Al Mansoori earned her Doctor of Medicine (MD) degree from George Washington University School of Medicine, where she completed both her residency and fellowship. In 2013, she became double board certified in Internal Medicine and Endocrinology and moved back to the UAE. She was presented at the 2014 World Government Summit by Deputy Prime Minister Sheikh Mansour Bin Zayed Al Nahyan as an example of success in education and innovation. Currently, she serves as the executive director of Intramural Research at Abu Dhabi Stem Cell Center and Deputy Medical Director of Imperial College London Diabetes Centre in Abu Dhabi.
achievements: First Emirati woman to receive UAE Presidential Scholarship for medicine
Double board certified in Internal Medicine and Endocrinology
UAE Pioneers Award recipient (2014)
Federal Personality of the year (2019)
Chairman and co-founder of SonoStik LLC

figures.name: Shamsa bint Suhail Al Mazrouei
figures.title: Sheikha
figures.roleDescription: Wife of Sheikh Khalifa bin Zayed Al Nahyan, President of the UAE
figures.significance: Prominent matriarch as the wife of UAE President Sheikh Khalifa bin Zayed Al Nahyan and mother of eight children who hold important positions in the UAE.
figures.biography:
Sheikha Shamsa bint Suhail Al Mazrouei was from the famous Al Khamis family of the Al Shikr branch of the Mazaria tribe in Liwa. She married Sheikh Khalifa bin Zayed Al Nahyan on October 18, 1964, becoming the wife of the future President of the United Arab Emirates and ruler of Abu Dhabi.

As the wife of Sheikh Khalifa, she became the mother of eight children who would go on to play significant roles in UAE society and politics. Her children include Sultan bin Khalifa Al Nahyan, Mohammed bin Khalifa Al Nahyan, and six daughters who married into prominent Emirati families, creating important dynastic connections within the Al Nahyan ruling family.
achievements: Mother of eight children of UAE President Sheikh Khalifa
Key matriarchal figure in Al Nahyan ruling family

figures.name: Mohamed Al Mazrouei
figures.title: Senior Government Official
figures.roleDescription: Senior aide to Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed and Manchester City board member
figures.significance: Senior aide to UAE President Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan and served on Manchester City's board during the club's financial fair play violations.
figures.biography:
Mohamed Al Mazrouei served as one of the two senior aides of Mohamed bin Zayed (MbZ) and worked as undersecretary at the Abu Dhabi Crown Prince's Court (CPC). He held significant influence in the UAE government structure and was closely involved in the Crown Prince's operations.

Al Mazrouei also served on Manchester City's board during a controversial period when the club was investigated for financial fair play violations. His dual role as a senior government official and football club board member highlighted the close connections between UAE leadership and the club's ownership under Sheikh Mansour bin Zayed Al Nahyan.
achievements: Senior aide to UAE President Mohamed bin Zayed
Board member of Manchester City F.C.

figures.name: Juma Mohammed Al Kaabi
figures.title: UAE Ambassador to Afghanistan
figures.roleDescription: Emirati career diplomat who served as UAE Ambassador to Afghanistan
figures.significance: Emirati diplomat who died from injuries sustained in the 2017 Afghanistan bombings while on a humanitarian mission.
figures.biography:
Juma Mohammed Al Kaabi was born in 1966 in the village of Wadi Al Qor in Ras Al Khaimah, United Arab Emirates. He was the eldest of his siblings, having 2 brothers and 6 sisters. His father died when he was 18 years old, and he became the primary caretaker of his family. After finishing high school, he joined the United Arab Emirates Armed Forces, then worked for the UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs for 8 years before being appointed as the UAE Ambassador to Afghanistan on July 28, 2016.

On January 10, 2017, Al Kaabi was on a humanitarian mission to Kandahar province, Afghanistan, to lay the foundation stone of an orphanage sponsored by the UAE government. A bombing occurred while he was at a dinner visit at the Kandahar governor's house, killing 13 people and injuring 18 others, including Al Kaabi. He was evacuated to Abu Dhabi and then transferred to a specialized hospital in France for treatment. On February 15, 2017, the Ministry of Presidential Affairs announced that Al Kaabi had died due to his injuries. He was married and had 3 daughters, 2 sons, and 2 grandchildren.
achievements: Served as UAE Ambassador to Afghanistan
Died while on humanitarian mission to open an orphanage
figures.birthPlace: Wadi Al Qor, Ras Al Khaimah, United Arab Emirates

figures.name: Ahmed Bin Na'aman Al-Kaabi
figures.title: Omani Emissary to the United States
figures.roleDescription: 19th century Omani diplomat who led the first Arab envoy to visit the New World
figures.significance: Led the historic first Arab diplomatic mission to the United States in 1840, strengthening Omani-American relations.
figures.biography:
Ahmed Bin Na'aman Al-Kaabi served as an emissary for Sultan Said bin Sultan of Oman during the mid-19th century. He commanded the ship Al-Sultanah, which docked at New York on April 13, 1840, making it the first Arab envoy to ever visit the New World. Her crew of fifty-six Arab sailors caused considerable excitement among the three hundred thousand residents of New York. The visit lasted nearly four months, during which Al-Kaabi and his officers were entertained by state and city dignitaries, received official resolutions, and were given tours of New York City.

Al-Sultanah carried ivory, Persian rugs, spices, coffee and dates, as well as lavish gifts for President Martin Van Buren. Among Bin Na'aman's hosts was Commodore Cornelius Vanderbilt, in whose home he met Governor William H. Seward and Vice President Richard Mentor Johnson. His portrait can still be seen in the Oman and Zanzibar display of the Peabody Museum in Massachusetts. When he prepared to leave, the United States completely repaired Al-Sultanah and presented him with gifts for his Sultan, demonstrating the success of this diplomatic mission.
achievements: Led first Arab diplomatic mission to the United States
Strengthened Omani-American relations through successful diplomatic visit

figures.name: Saad Sherida al-Kaabi
figures.title: Qatar Energy Minister
figures.roleDescription: Current Qatar Energy Minister who succeeded Mohammed Saleh Al Sada
figures.significance: Current Qatar Energy Minister who has played a key role in Qatar's energy sector and made significant statements during the 2026 Iran war regarding global energy security.
figures.biography:
Saad Sherida al-Kaabi serves as Qatar's Energy Minister, having succeeded Mohammed Saleh Al Sada in this role. He has been instrumental in overseeing Qatar's vast energy resources and managing the country's position as a major global supplier of liquefied natural gas. During the 2026 Iran war, al-Kaabi made significant statements about the potential global economic impact of the conflict on energy markets.

In March 2026, during the Iran conflict, al-Kaabi warned that if the war continued, other Gulf energy producers might be forced to halt exports and declare force majeure, stating that 'this will bring down economies of the world.' His warnings came after QatarEnergy announced it was declaring Force Majeure on its contracts with buyers following reported drone attacks on Qatari gas facilities. Al-Kaabi's statements highlighted the critical importance of Gulf energy exports to the global economy and the potential catastrophic consequences of prolonged regional conflicts on worldwide energy security.
achievements: Current Qatar Energy Minister
Made significant statements on global energy security during regional conflicts

figures.name: Muhammad bin Awad bin Laden
figures.title: Founder and Patriarch
figures.roleDescription: Founder of the bin Laden family fortune and construction empire
figures.significance: Founded what is today the Saudi Binladin Group and became the wealthiest non-royal Saudi, establishing the wealth and prestige of the bin Laden family.
figures.biography:
Muhammad bin Awad bin Laden was born around 1908 on the Hadramawt coast of south Yemen to Awad, a Kindite Hadrami tribesman from al-Rubat, a village in Wadi Doan. Poor and uneducated, he emigrated to Saudi Arabia before World War I, initially working as a porter in Jeddah. In 1931, he started his own construction business and came to the attention of Abdul Aziz Ibn Saud through construction projects. He made his initial fortune from exclusive rights to construct all mosques and other religious buildings not only in Saudi Arabia, but as far as Ibn Saud's influence reached.

Muhammad strategically built strong ties with the Saudi royal family, notably constructing a ramp at the palace in Jeddah to help the aging king move between floors. In 1964, King Faisal issued a royal decree awarding all future construction projects to Muhammad's company, helping the company amass assets exceeding $5 billion. He fathered 54 children by 22 wives, following Islamic polygyny principles. On September 3, 1967, Muhammad was killed when his airplane crashed during landing in Usran, 'Asir Province. His funeral drew nearly 10,000 people, and King Faisal placed his children under royal protection.
achievements: Founded the Saudi Binladin Group construction empire
Secured exclusive rights to construct all mosques in Saudi Arabia
Built strong ties with Saudi royal family through major construction projects
Amassed company assets exceeding $5 billion

figures.name: Salem bin Laden
figures.title: Chairman and Head of Family Business
figures.roleDescription: Succeeded his father as head of the family construction empire
figures.significance: Took over the family empire in 1967 upon his father's death and expanded it into an international company.
figures.biography:
Salem bin Laden attended Millfield, an English boarding school, and took over the family empire in 1967 upon the death of his father Muhammad. He was also an amateur rock guitarist in the 1970s and married an English art student, Caroline Carey, whose half-brother was the son of the Marquess of Queensberry. Under his leadership, the family business was renamed 'Binladen Brothers for Contracting and Industry' and continued to expand, eventually employing more than 40,000 people.

Salem died outside San Antonio, Texas in 1988 when an experimental ultralight plane that he was flying got tangled in power lines. His death marked the end of an era for the family business, which would later be taken over by his brother Bakr bin Laden and renamed the Saudi Binladin Group in May 1990.
achievements: Expanded family construction business internationally
Grew company to employ over 40,000 people
Renamed company to 'Binladen Brothers for Contracting and Industry'

figures.name: Bakr bin Laden
figures.title: Chairman of Saudi Binladin Group
figures.roleDescription: Current leader of the family construction empire
figures.significance: Succeeded Salem as chairman of the Saudi Binladin Group and is described as a major power broker in Jeddah.
figures.biography:
Bakr bin Laden succeeded Salem as the chairman of the Saudi Binladin Group after Salem's death in 1988. In May 1990, the company was renamed the Saudi Binladin Group under his leadership. He is described as a major power broker in Jeddah and has been reported to 'truly' rule the city, where the headquarters of the Saudi Binladin Group is located.

Under Bakr's leadership, the Saudi Binladin Group expanded beyond construction into engineering, manufacturing, and telecommunications. The company undertook major projects including airports, housing complexes, tunnels, and bridges, and became involved in city planning and real estate development. The group also became Egypt's largest private foreign company and negotiated with the Lebanese government to rebuild part of central Beirut under a $50 million contract. Bakr occasionally dines with his nephew Abdullah bin Laden at the Intercontinental Hotel in Jeddah.
achievements: Led Saudi Binladin Group expansion into multiple industries
Oversaw company's growth as Egypt's largest private foreign investor
Negotiated $50 million Beirut reconstruction contract

figures.name: Osama bin Laden
figures.title: Terrorist Leader
figures.roleDescription: Founder and leader of al-Qaeda terrorist organization
figures.significance: Co-founded the terrorism group Al-Qaeda and was responsible for attacks including the September 11 attacks.
figures.biography:
Osama bin Laden was born in 1957 in Saudi Arabia, the 17th child of Muhammad bin Awad bin Laden and the only son of his tenth wife, Hamida al-Attas, who was of Syrian origin. His parents divorced soon after his birth, and his mother was given in marriage to one of his father's company executives around 1958. Osama was raised in the household of his stepfather Muhammad al-Attas in Jeddah. In 1994, the bin Laden family publicly disowned him, and the Saudi government revoked his passport and citizenship for speaking out against the government for permitting U.S. troops to be based in Saudi Arabia.

Osama founded al-Qaeda and became responsible for numerous terrorist attacks, most notably the September 11, 2001 attacks on the United States. He lived in exile in Sudan and Afghanistan before ultimately hiding in a compound in Abbottabad, Pakistan. He was killed on May 2, 2011, during a U.S. Navy SEAL operation at his compound. He had multiple wives and over 20 children, and was one of the FBI's Most Wanted Terrorists until his death.

figures.name: Omar bin Laden
figures.title: Artist and Cultural Ambassador
figures.roleDescription: Saudi artist, author, and businessman who publicly disavowed his father's actions
figures.significance: The only son of Osama bin Laden to publicly disavow his father's actions and became an advocate for peace.
figures.biography:
Omar bin Laden was born on March 1, 1981, as the fourth-eldest son of Osama bin Laden and his first wife Najwa Ghanhem. He accompanied his father during exile to Sudan and then to Afghanistan, and trained in al-Qaeda camps beginning at age 14. After six years of training and sharing a house with al-Qaeda's second-in-command Ayman al-Zawahiri, Omar left al-Qaeda in 2000 because he disagreed with killing civilians, with his father not objecting to his departure.

Omar returned to Saudi Arabia and ran his own company in Jeddah as a contractor and scrap metal dealer. In 2006, he married Jane Felix-Browne (Zaina Mohamed Al-Sabah), a British parish councillor 24 years his senior. He has been denied visas to the UK and Europe, and has sought asylum in various countries. Omar co-authored the book 'Growing Up bin Laden' with his mother in 2009, detailing their harsh life under Osama's control. He has consistently advocated for peace and condemned his father's terrorist activities while expressing personal love for him as his father.
achievements: Co-authored 'Growing Up bin Laden' memoir
Became advocate for peace between Muslims and the West
Only son of Osama bin Laden to publicly disavow father's actions

figures.name: Hamza bin Laden
figures.title: Al-Qaeda Member
figures.roleDescription: Key member of al-Qaeda and son of Osama bin Laden
figures.significance: Was considered a key leader of Al-Qaeda and was being groomed as his father's potential successor before being killed in a U.S. counterterrorism operation.
figures.biography:
Hamza bin Laden was born on May 9, 1989, in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, the son of Osama bin Laden and Khairiah Sabir. In January 2001, he attended his brother Mohammed's wedding in Kandahar, Afghanistan, and video footage from November 2001 shows 12-year-old Hamza handling U.S. helicopter wreckage alongside the Taliban. From March 2002 to July or August 2010, Hamza was detained in Iran under prison-like conditions during his adolescence, from age 12 to 21.

After his release, Hamza moved to Qatar for several years to study Islamic religious texts as his father had recommended, then returned to Pakistan around 2015. Between 2015 and 2017, he released audio and video messages calling for attacks against Western targets. The U.S. classified him as a Specially Designated Global Terrorist in January 2017 and offered a $1 million reward for information on his whereabouts in February 2019. Saudi Arabia revoked his citizenship in March 2019. On September 14, 2019, President Donald Trump confirmed that Hamza had been killed in a U.S. counterterrorism operation in the Afghanistan-Pakistan region, though some 2024 reports claim he may still be alive.

figures.name: Yeslam bin Laden
figures.title: Business Executive
figures.roleDescription: Geneva-based head of family's European operations
figures.significance: Head of the family's European holding company and became a Swiss citizen, representing the international expansion of bin Laden business interests.
figures.biography:
Yeslam bin Laden was born in 1950 and studied in the 1970s at the University of Southern California in Los Angeles. He later settled in Switzerland and became a Swiss citizen in 2001. Yeslam serves as the Geneva-based head of the family's European holding company, the Saudi Investment Company, representing the bin Laden family's international business interests beyond the Middle East.

Yeslam was scrutinized by Swiss and American investigators because of a financial stake he held in a Swiss aviation firm. He has claimed to not have had contact with his half-brother Osama since 1981, distancing himself from Osama's terrorist activities. Yeslam married Carmen Dufour and has three daughters: Wafah Dufour (born 1975), who became an American model and aspiring singer-songwriter, Najia Dufour (born 1981), and Noor Dufour (born 1987). The family lived in Geneva, and after Yeslam and Carmen separated in 1988, the children remained primarily in Switzerland.
achievements: Established European operations for bin Laden business interests
Obtained Swiss citizenship while maintaining family business ties
Studied at University of Southern California

figures.name: Abdullah bin Osama bin Laden
figures.title: Businessman
figures.roleDescription: Eldest son of Osama bin Laden who runs advertising firm in Jeddah
figures.significance: The eldest son of Osama bin Laden who completely broke with his father and established his own business in Saudi Arabia.
figures.biography:
Abdullah bin Osama bin Laden was born in 1976 as the eldest son of Osama bin Laden and Najwa Ghanem. Although his father initially showed him favor, taking him to Afghanistan in 1984 when he was only eight, Abdullah later became disgusted with life in Sudan. He moved back to Jeddah to marry his first cousin and refused to rejoin his father in Sudan, except for a brief, unpleasant visit. Abdullah never saw or communicated with his father again after 1995.

Abdullah now resides in Jeddah where he runs his own firm called Fame Advertising. He has been closely watched by the Saudi government, which restricted his travel from the kingdom after 1996, at least for a time. He has stated that the media have misrepresented his father and is known to dine occasionally with his uncle, Saudi Binladin Group chairman Bakr bin Laden, at the Intercontinental Hotel in Jeddah. In 2015, WikiLeaks revealed that Abdullah had requested his father's death certificate from the U.S. embassy in Saudi Arabia, but was told that no death certificate was issued for Osama.
achievements: Founded Fame Advertising company in Jeddah
Successfully distanced himself from father's terrorist activities
Maintained business operations despite family scrutiny

figures.name: Abdulaziz bin Abdullah Al Zamil
figures.title: Minister of Industry and Electricity
figures.roleDescription: Industrial engineer, SABIC CEO, and government minister
figures.significance: Industrial engineer whose work was instrumental in the industrial development of Saudi Arabia as the original CEO of SABIC and Minister of Industry and Electricity.
figures.biography:
Abdulaziz bin Abdullah Al Zamil was born in 1942 in Bahrain, the fourth of twelve sons of Abdullah Al-Hamad Al Zamil. He completed his education in Great Britain and received his bachelor's and master's degrees in industrial engineering from the University of Southern California in 1967 and 1968 respectively. Returning to Saudi Arabia in 1968, he worked at the Saudi Consulting House before being asked in 1975 by Minister Ghazi Abdul Rahman Al Gosaibi to form Saudi Basic Industries Corporation (SABIC).

He served as Vice Chairman and Chief Executive of SABIC from 1976 to 1983, helping negotiate joint-venture agreements with leading foreign chemical companies and overseeing the training of Saudi employees. In 1983, he was appointed Minister of Industry and Electricity and became Chairman of SABIC, serving until 1995. During his tenure, SABIC's manufacturing operations expanded significantly, producing various petrochemicals and industrial products. After retiring from government in 1995, he joined the family business Zamil Group Holding Company and established the Saudi International Petrochemical Company (Sipchem).
achievements: Founded and led SABIC as original CEO (1976-1983)
Served as Minister of Industry and Electricity (1983-1995)
Received King Abdul Aziz Medal (1984)
Received Richard J. Bolte Sr. Award for supporting industries (2015)
Chairman of Alinma Bank

figures.name: Abdullah Al-Hamad Al Zamil
figures.title: Merchant and Founder
figures.roleDescription: Founder of the Zamil family business empire
figures.significance: Originally from Onaiza in Central Arabia, he established a food and textile trading entity in Bahrain in the 1930s, founding the Zamil family business empire.
figures.biography:
Abdullah Al-Hamad Al Zamil was originally from Onaiza, Central Arabia, and established a food and textile trading entity in Bahrain in the 1930s. He later expanded his business interests into real estate investment. He was the father of twelve sons, including Abdulaziz bin Abdullah Al Zamil who would later become a prominent industrialist and government minister.

Upon his death in 1961, the family chose to continue operation of his business as a group under the leadership of his eldest son, Mohammed. This decision laid the foundation for what would become the Zamil Group Holding Company, which now employs more than 12,000 people in 60 countries and is involved in diverse sectors including general construction, paints, plastics, petrochemicals, shipbuilding, and port management.
achievements: Established trading entity in Bahrain in the 1930s
Founded the Zamil family business empire
Expanded into real estate investment

figures.name: Abdulrahman Abdullah Al Zamil
figures.title: Business Executive
figures.roleDescription: Co-founder of Saudi International Petrochemical Company
figures.significance: Brother of Abdulaziz Al Zamil who co-founded the Saudi International Petrochemical Company (Sipchem) and completed his final year of high school in Kuwait in 1959.
figures.biography: Abdulrahman Abdullah Al Zamil was one of the twelve sons of Abdullah Al-Hamad Al Zamil, the founder of the Zamil family business. He completed his final year of high school in 1959 in Kuwait together with his brother Abdulaziz. As part of the Zamil Group's expansion into petrochemicals after 1995, he worked alongside his brother Abdulaziz to establish the Saudi International Petrochemical Company (Sipchem), where Abdulaziz served as Chairman.
achievements: Co-founded Saudi International Petrochemical Company (Sipchem)

figures.name: Mohammed Abdullah Al Zamil
figures.title: Business Leader
figures.roleDescription: Eldest son and leader of Zamil Group after father's death
figures.significance: The eldest son of Abdullah Al-Hamad Al Zamil who led the family business group after his father's death in 1961.
figures.biography: Mohammed Abdullah Al Zamil was the eldest son of Abdullah Al-Hamad Al Zamil, the founder of the Zamil family business empire. Upon his father's death in 1961, the family chose to continue operation of the business as a group under Mohammed's leadership. This decision established the foundation for the continued growth and expansion of what would become the Zamil Group Holding Company, ensuring the family business continued as a unified entity rather than being divided among the twelve sons.
achievements: Led Zamil Group after father's death in 1961

figures.name: Mohammed Ibrahim Al Subeaei
figures.title: Significant Stakeholder
figures.roleDescription: Co-founder and major stakeholder of Bank Albilad
figures.significance: Co-established one of Saudi Arabia's prominent Islamic banks in 2004.
figures.biography:
Mohammed Ibrahim Al Subeaei is one of the founding figures of Bank Albilad, an Islamic Saudi bank established in 2004. Along with his brother Abdullah, he became a significant stakeholder in the institution, helping to establish what would become a major player in Saudi Arabia's Islamic banking sector.

Bank Albilad operates as a Saudi joint stock company headquartered in Riyadh, offering products and services compliant with Sharia (Islamic) law. The bank provides retail banking products, business products, and investment services through its investment arm "Albilad Capital," as well as international remittance services through "Enjaz." The institution has grown significantly since its establishment, with shareholders approving an increase in capitalization to 10 billion Saudi Riyals in May 2022.
achievements: Co-founded Bank Albilad in 2004
Established significant stakeholder position in major Islamic bank

figures.name: Abdullah Ibrahim Al Subeaei
figures.title: Significant Stakeholder
figures.roleDescription: Co-founder and major stakeholder of Bank Albilad
figures.significance: Co-established one of Saudi Arabia's prominent Islamic banks in 2004.
figures.biography:
Abdullah Ibrahim Al Subeaei is a co-founding figure of Bank Albilad, working alongside his brother Mohammed to establish the Islamic Saudi bank in 2004. As a significant stakeholder, he played a crucial role in the bank's formation and development into a major financial institution in the Kingdom.

The bank he helped establish has become a prominent player in Saudi Arabia's Islamic banking sector, offering Sharia-compliant financial services including retail banking, business products, and investment services. Bank Albilad operates as a joint stock company based in Riyadh and has demonstrated substantial growth, with capitalization reaching 10 billion Saudi Riyals by 2022.
achievements: Co-founded Bank Albilad in 2004
Established significant stakeholder position in major Islamic bank

figures.name: Nasser Mohammed Al-Subeaei
figures.title: Chairman of the Board of Directors
figures.roleDescription: Current Chairman of Bank Albilad's Board of Directors
figures.significance: Leads the governance of one of Saudi Arabia's major Islamic banks as Chairman.
figures.biography:
Nasser Mohammed Al-Subeaei currently serves as the Chairman of the Board of Directors of Bank Albilad, having been appointed to this position in February 2021. His appointment represents continued family leadership in the institution that was founded by other members of the Al Subeaei family.

As Chairman, he oversees the strategic direction of Bank Albilad, which operates as a Saudi joint stock company providing Islamic banking services compliant with Sharia law. Under the board's guidance, the bank has continued to expand its services and capitalize on growth opportunities, including the significant capitalization increase to 10 billion Saudi Riyals approved by shareholders in May 2022. The bank offers comprehensive financial services including retail banking, business products, investment services through Albilad Capital, and remittance services through Enjaz.
achievements: Appointed Chairman of Bank Albilad Board of Directors in February 2021
Leads governance of major Saudi Islamic bank

figures.name: Mariam bint Ali bin Nasser Al Misnad
figures.nameAr: مريم بنت علي بن ناصر المسند
figures.title: Minister of State for International Cooperation
figures.roleDescription: Current Minister of State for International Cooperation at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Qatar
figures.significance: First female Qatari minister to visit Afghanistan on a humanitarian mission since the Taliban takeover in 2021.
figures.biography:
Mariam bint Ali bin Nasser Al Misnad is a prominent Qatari politician who has held several high-ranking government positions. She was appointed Minister of State for International Cooperation at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in November 2024, following her previous role as Minister of Social Development and Family from October 2021 to November 2024. She also serves as vice-chairperson of the Board of Directors of the Qatar Fund for Development (QFFD).

Al Misnad holds impressive academic credentials, including a Bachelor's degree in Humanities and English Literature from Qatar University, an Executive Master's Degree in Strategic Planning and Business Administration from HEC Paris (2014), and a PhD in Public Administration from the University of Hassan the Second (2025). Her career spans various social development and humanitarian organizations, including roles at the Supreme Council for Family Affairs, Education Above All Foundation, and the Orphans Care Center 'Dreama' where she served as Executive Director from 2013 to 2021.

In September 2025, following the Kunar earthquake, Al-Misnad made history by traveling to Kabul to oversee the delivery of humanitarian aid by the Qatari government, becoming the first female minister to visit Afghanistan on a humanitarian mission since the Taliban takeover in 2021. She also serves as president of the advisory board of the center for conflict and humanitarian studies and is a member of the board of trustees of the Qatar Museum Authority.
achievements: First female minister to visit Afghanistan on humanitarian mission since Taliban takeover (2021)
International Ambassador for Orphans by the Committee of Sanabil Award for Social Responsibility (2019)
International Social Responsibility Excellence Award (2021)
Chaired coordination meeting of Arab Ministers of Social Affairs at UN Headquarters (2023)

figures.name: Moza bint Nasser Al-Missned
figures.title: Sheikha and Chair of Qatar Foundation
figures.roleDescription: Co-founder and chair of the Qatar Foundation, consort of former Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani
figures.significance: Influential figure in Qatar's education and social development, mother of current Emir Sheikh Tamim, and major force behind Qatar's soft power strategy.
figures.biography:
Sheikha Moza bint Nasser Al-Missned was born on August 8, 1959, daughter of Nasser bin Abdullah Al-Missned, a well-known opposition activist and former head of the Al-Muhannada confederation of Bani Hajer. She spent much of her childhood in Kuwait during her father's exile following his imprisonment for political activities against the policies of the deposed emir Ahmad bin Ali Al Thani. She married Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, the heir apparent of Qatar, in 1977, the same year her father returned to Qatar. She is one of his three wives and the mother of seven children, including the current Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani.

Sheikha Moza received a BA in Sociology from Qatar University in 1986 and holds an MA in Public Policy in Islam from Hamad Bin Khalifa University. She has received honorary doctorates from prestigious institutions including Virginia Commonwealth University, Texas A&M, Carnegie Mellon, Imperial College London, and Georgetown University. She co-founded the Qatar Foundation for Education, Science and Community Development in 1995 and has been instrumental in establishing Education City in Doha, bringing Western universities to Qatar as part of the country's soft power strategy.

She has held numerous influential positions including UNESCO's Special Envoy for Basic and Higher Education (2003-2023), chairperson of Silatech since 2008, and founder of the Supreme Council for Family Affairs in 1998. She established Education Above All in 2012, focusing on making education accessible to marginalized children globally. She has been recognized as one of the '100 Most Powerful Women' by Forbes and has significant political influence in shaping Qatar's international relations and domestic policies.
achievements: Co-founder and chair of Qatar Foundation (1995)
Established Education Above All foundation (2012)
UNESCO Special Envoy for Basic and Higher Education (2003-2023)
Listed among '100 Most Powerful Women' by Forbes (2007, 2010)
Endowed Sidra Medical and Research Center with $7.9 billion

figures.name: Nasser bin Abdullah Al-Missned
figures.title: Opposition Activist and Tribal Leader
figures.roleDescription: Former head of the Al-Muhannada confederation of Bani Hajer and prominent opposition activist
figures.significance: Notable opposition activist against the policies of deposed Emir Ahmad bin Ali Al Thani and father of Sheikha Moza.
figures.biography:
Nasser bin Abdullah Al-Missned was a well-known opposition activist and former head of the Al-Muhannada confederation of Bani Hajer. His political activities led to his imprisonment and subsequent exile due to his defiance against the policies of the deposed emir Ahmad bin Ali Al Thani. During his exile, his family, including his daughter Moza, lived in Kuwait.

He returned to Qatar with his immediate family in 1977, the same year his daughter Moza married Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, who was then the heir apparent of Qatar. His political activism and opposition stance during the earlier period of Qatar's modern history positioned him as a significant figure in the country's political landscape, and his legacy continues through his daughter's influential role in Qatari society.
achievements: Former head of Al-Muhannada confederation of Bani Hajer
Prominent opposition activist against royal policies

figures.name: Saeed bin Abdullah Al Misnad
figures.title: Former CEO of Qatar National Bank
figures.roleDescription: Former chief executive of Qatar National Bank, succeeded by Ali Sharif Al Emadi in 2005
figures.significance: Led Qatar National Bank as chief executive until 2005, contributing to the bank's development during his tenure.
figures.biography:
Saeed bin Abdullah Al Misnad served as the chief executive of Qatar National Bank, one of the region's leading financial institutions. He held this position until 2005, when he was succeeded by Ali Sharif Al Emadi. His tenure at the bank contributed to its growth and development during a crucial period in Qatar's economic expansion.

As a member of the Al Misnad family, he represented the family's involvement in Qatar's financial sector. His role at Qatar National Bank positioned him as an important figure in the country's banking and business community during his years of service.
achievements: Former chief executive of Qatar National Bank

figures.name: Shaikha Rodha Al-Misnad
figures.title: Royal Family Member
figures.roleDescription: Wife of Sheikh Fahad bin Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, member of the ruling family through marriage
figures.significance: Connected the Al Misnad family to the ruling Al Thani family through her marriage to Sheikh Fahad.
figures.biography:
Shaikha Rodha Al-Misnad is married to Sheikh Fahad bin Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, who is the second son of former Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani. Through this marriage, she connects the Al Misnad merchant family with the ruling Al Thani family, representing an important alliance between prominent Qatari families.

She and Sheikh Fahad have four children together: one son, Shaikh Hamad bin Fahad Al-Hamad Al-Thani, and three daughters: Shaikha Mariam, Shaikha Asma, and Shaikha Nayla bint Fahad Al-Hamad Al-Thani. Her marriage represents the continued importance and social standing of the Al Misnad family within Qatar's elite circles.

figures.name: Khalid Bin Nasser Bin Abdulla Al Misnad
figures.nameAr: خالد بن ناصر بن عبد الله المسند
figures.title: President of the Misnad Group
figures.roleDescription: President of the Misnad Group, an international trading and construction company
figures.significance: Led the Misnad Group as an international trading and construction company and was involved in significant legal proceedings in Australia.
figures.biography:
Khalid Bin Nasser Bin Abdulla Al Misnad serves as the president of the Misnad Group, described as an international trading and construction company. This positions him as a key figure in the Al Misnad family's business operations and their involvement in international commerce and construction projects.

He became notable in legal proceedings when he, along with Omar Ali Babtain, lodged a challenge against Adel Flaifel with the Supreme Court in Brisbane, Australia. They claimed that Flaifel had sold them properties in the Middle East worth 59.5 million Australian dollars but never transferred the contracts, and had used that money to purchase Australian properties. This case highlighted the Al Misnad family's international business dealings and their involvement in significant commercial transactions across different countries.
achievements: President of the Misnad Group international trading and construction company
Involved in major international legal proceedings in Australia

figures.name: Abdullatif bin Rashid Al Zayani
figures.title: Foreign Minister of Bahrain
figures.roleDescription: Former Secretary General of GCC and current Foreign Minister
figures.significance: First military background Secretary General of the GCC and current Foreign Minister of Bahrain who signed the Bahrain-Israel normalization agreement.
figures.biography:
Abdullatif bin Rashid Al Zayani was born on 15 April 1954 in Muharraq, Kingdom of Bahrain. He graduated from the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst in the United Kingdom and received advanced degrees including a PhD in operations research from the Naval Postgraduate School, Monterey, California in 1986. He became a commissioned officer in the Bahrain Defence Force in 1973, rising through the ranks to lieutenant general in 2010 before his military career ended on 2 June 2004.

Zayani served as Secretary General of the Gulf Cooperation Council from 1 April 2011 to February 2020, becoming the fifth GCC secretary general and the first with military background since the GCC was established. In January 2020, he was appointed as Bahrain's Foreign Minister, replacing Khalid bin Ahmed Al Khalifa.
